configurations.name: nanoclaw-dev
configurations.description: Main NanoClaw orchestrator — hot-reload via tsx. Hosts the mini-app (3847), trust-gateway (10255), webhook (8090), and browser-sidecar subprocesses. Note: conflicts with the production launchd instance — stop it first with `launchctl unload ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.nanoclaw.plist`.

configurations.name: nanoclaw-build
configurations.description: One-shot TypeScript compile (tsc) — not a server, useful for verifying changes before committing.

configurations.name: mini-app
configurations.description: Telegram Mini App server (Express). Serves email/task/draft-diff routes. Normally started inside the main NanoClaw process, but can be run standalone for UI work.

configurations.name: miniapp-preview
configurations.description: Standalone mini-app preview with seeded in-memory DB. Used for local UI verification of Attention queue changes.

configurations.name: brain-preview
configurations.description: Mini-app preview pointing at the production brain.db with Telegram auth disabled. Used to verify /brain/* UI changes against real data. The script chdirs to the main repo at startup so STORE_DIR resolves to the production store/ directory.

configurations.name: miniapp-tunnel
configurations.description: Cloudflare tunnel exposing localhost:3847 as https://miniapp.inboxsuperpilot.com.

configurations.name: whatsapp-auth
configurations.description: One-shot WhatsApp pairing worker (pairing code mode). Run only when re-authenticating.

configurations.name: test-watch
configurations.description: Vitest in watch mode — re-runs unit tests on file changes.

configurations.name: webhook-server
configurations.description: Standalone webhook event source (GitHub/Notion/generic). Normally embedded in NanoClaw; this entry is for reference.

configurations.name: trust-gateway
configurations.description: Trust gateway for container-to-host HTTPS proxy. Normally embedded in NanoClaw; reference entry.
